ordinary_no_progress_guard.fingerprint: ordinary|that error happened because the block is **json**, but it was pasted directly into **powershell**. powershell sees `"googlecloudcode.project":` and treats `:` as invalid syntax. use this powershell-safe version instead: ```powershell $settingspath = join-path $env:appdata "code\user\settings.json" new-item -itemtype directory -force -path (split-path $settingspath) | out-null if (test-path $settingspath) { $raw = get-content $settingspath -raw $settings = if ($raw.trim()) { $raw | convertfrom-json -ashashtable } else { @{} } } else { $settings = @{} } $settings["googlecloudcode.project"] = "euphoric-axon-500505-f9" $settings["editor.formatonsave"] = $true $settings["editor.codeactionsonsave"] = @{ "source.fixall" = "explicit" } $settings["extensions.autoupdate"] = $true $settings["extensions.ignorerecommendations"] = $false $settings["security.workspace.trust.enabled"] = $true $settings["telemetry.telemetrylevel"] = "error" $settings["terminal.integrated.defaultprofile.windows"] = "powershell" $settings | convertto-json -depth 20 | set-content -path $settingspath -encoding utf8 code --reuse-window $settingspath ``` then set the active google cloud project too: ```powershell gcloud config set project euphoric-axon-500505-f9 ``` alternative: open vs code settings json with `ctrl+shift+p` → `preferences: open user settings (json)` and paste the json inside the existing `{ ... }`, not into powershell.
